text.title: C4 Model for Architecture Visualization Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how c4-model produces a valid spec artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- artefact_id: c4-pack-shop-2026-05-23 owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> toolchain: structurizr ---
## L1 — Context - System: Shop - Actors: Customer, Admin, Operator - External: Stripe (payments), SendGrid (mail), Tempo (tracing)
## L2 — Containers | Container | Technology | Description | |-----------|------------|-------------| | Web App | React 18 | SPA for customers + admins | | API | Django 5 | Business logic + REST API | | Worker | Celery | Async jobs | | DB | PostgreSQL 16 | Primary store | | Cache | Redis 7 | Sessions + hot path |
## L3 — Components (API only) - `application/place_order` - `application/refund_order` - `adapters/http/orders_view` - `adapters/db/order_repo`
## Sync policy - Diagrams updated in every PR that adds/removes a container or external system. - CI: `python scripts/c4_sync_check.py` compares L2 against deploy/manifest.yaml.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
